question: Show each department with the number of employees formed by adding its faculty count and staff count.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT "departments"."department_name", SUM("departments"."faculty_count") AS "total_employees", SUM("departments"."staff_count") AS "total_employees" FROM "departments" GROUP BY "departments"."department_name"
reason_selected_wrong: Selected llm_primary sums faculty_count and staff_count into two columns sharing one alias; it never adds them.
correct_candidate_sql: SELECT department_name, faculty_count, staff_count, faculty_count + staff_count AS number_of_employees FROM departments GROUP BY department_id
reason_correct_candidate_lost: cand6 computes faculty_count + staff_count (score 93, ABOVE the selected 85) and executed cleanly, but the correlated 3-member llm_primary/variant family won consensus and the direct family's effective score was its base (78), so the computing candidate was not chosen.
audit_reason: Returns separate faculty and staff sums with the same alias instead of adding faculty_count and staff_count.

question: Show each department with the number of unused beds based on bed capacity minus current occupancy.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT department_name, bed_capacity, current_occupancy FROM departments
reason_selected_wrong: Selected llm_sql_direct returns bed_capacity and current_occupancy as plain columns (score 73); it never subtracts.
correct_candidate_sql: SELECT department_name, bed_capacity, current_occupancy, (bed_capacity - current_occupancy) AS unused_beds FROM departments GROUP BY department_id
reason_correct_candidate_lost: cand4/5/6 compute (bed_capacity - current_occupancy) AS unused_beds (score 88) and repair also reached 88, yet best_scored_executed kept the lower-scored plain-column direct candidate.
audit_reason: Sums bed_capacity instead of calculating bed_capacity - current_occupancy.

question: Show each department with its annual budget per enrolled department student.
day2_selected_sql: WITH dept_agg AS ( SELECT department_id, SUM(annual_budget) AS total_annual_budget, SUM(student_count) AS total_student_count FROM departments GROUP BY department_id ) SELECT d.department_name, d.annual_budget, d.student_count FROM departments d JOIN dept_agg da ON d.department_id = da.department_id GROUP BY d.department_id HAVING da.total_student_count > 0
reason_selected_wrong: Selected llm_sql_repair returns annual_budget and student_count but the final projection performs no division (score 100).
correct_candidate_sql: SELECT d.department_name, d.annual_budget, d.student_count, CAST(d.annual_budget AS REAL) / NULLIF(d.student_count, 0) AS annual_budget_per_student FROM departments d GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name, d.annual_budget, d.student_count
reason_correct_candidate_lost: cand4/5/6 compute CAST(annual_budget AS REAL)/NULLIF(student_count,0) (score 82); the repair candidate was over-rewarded to 100 while the ratio candidates were under-scored, so the dividing candidate lost. (Denominator column student_count is a defensible reading of 'enrolled department student'.)
audit_reason: Returns annual budget and enrollment totals but never divides budget by enrolled students.

question: List product categories with an average markup percentage above 40 percent.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT "products"."category", AVG("sales_order_items"."unit_price") AS "avg_price", AVG("sales_order_items"."unit_cost") AS "avg_cost" FROM "products" INNER JOIN "sales_order_items" ON "products"."product_id" = "sales_order_items"."product_id" GROUP BY "products"."category" HAVING "avg_price" > ?
reason_selected_wrong: Selected llm_variant tests AVG(unit_price) > threshold and never computes or tests a markup percentage (score 100).
correct_candidate_sql: SELECT p.category FROM products p JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.product_id = soi.product_id GROUP BY p.category HAVING AVG((CAST(soi.unit_price AS REAL) - CAST(soi.unit_cost AS REAL)) / NULLIF(CAST(soi.unit_cost AS REAL), 0)) * 100 > 40
reason_correct_candidate_lost: cand4/5/6 compute HAVING AVG((unit_price-unit_cost)/unit_cost)*100 > 40 (score 100, TIED with the wrong candidate); the tie-break/order selected the non-computing candidate because the scorer cannot separate them.
audit_reason: Checks only average unit price > average unit cost; it never calculates or tests a 40% markup.

question: List suppliers whose annual revenue is above the overall supplier average.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT "suppliers"."supplier_name", AVG("suppliers"."annual_revenue") AS "avg_revenue" FROM "suppliers" HAVING "avg_revenue" > ?
reason_selected_wrong: Selected llm_variant aggregates the whole table with AVG in HAVING and returns 0 rows (score 62).
correct_candidate_sql: SELECT s.supplier_id, s.supplier_name, s.annual_revenue FROM suppliers s WHERE s.annual_revenue > (SELECT AVG(annual_revenue) FROM suppliers)
fatal_or_rejection_reason: soft-rejected (scored, not fatal): grain violation: contract requires SUM(suppliers.annual_revenue) per suppliers.supplier_id, but the SQL compares the raw row-level value suppliers.annual_revenue and never computes SUM(annual_revenue) | grain violation: the comparison uses AVG(suppliers.annual_revenue) over raw rows where a SUM of per-entity totals per suppliers.supplier_id is required — an average of raw rows is a different scale than a total of per-entity SUMs
reason_correct_candidate_lost: cand4-7 use WHERE annual_revenue > (SELECT AVG(annual_revenue) FROM suppliers) (31 rows) but a grain semantic-contract false-positive ('requires SUM(annual_revenue) per supplier_id') penalised every correct candidate to score 29, so the wrong aggregate outranked them.
audit_reason: Aggregates the entire supplier table with HAVING and does not compare each supplier's revenue with the overall average.

question: Show each doctor with the number of years remaining until license expiration.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT doctor_id, first_name, last_name, license_expiration_date FROM doctors
reason_selected_wrong: Selected llm_sql_repair (via consensus_group) returns doctor columns with NO years_remaining expression (score 68).
correct_candidate_sql: SELECT doctor_id, first_name, last_name, license_expiration_date, CAST((julianday(license_expiration_date) - julianday('now')) / 365.25 AS INTEGER) AS years_remaining FROM doctors
reason_correct_candidate_lost: cand3/cand4 compute (julianday(license_expiration_date)-julianday('now'))/365.25 for all 80 doctors (score 68, tied); consensus_group chose the no-computation repair over the computing candidates.
audit_reason: Adds an unrequested active-doctor filter, so it does not show each doctor.

question: How many distinct programs have students advised by instructors from the same department as the program?
day2_selected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.program_id) FROM programs p JOIN students s ON p.program_id = s.program_id JOIN instructors i ON s.advisor_instructor_id = i.instructor_id JOIN departments d ON i.department_id = d.department_id JOIN academic_advising aa ON s.student_id = aa.student_id WHERE i.department_id = p.department_id
reason_selected_wrong: Selected llm_sql_direct_grain adds an INNER JOIN academic_advising, imposing an unrequested advising-appointment restriction (score 100).
correct_candidate_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.program_id) FROM programs p JOIN students s ON p.program_id = s.program_id JOIN instructors i ON s.advisor_instructor_id = i.instructor_id JOIN departments dp ON i.department_id = dp.department_id JOIN departments dp2 ON p.department_id = dp2.department_id WHERE i.department_id = p.department_id
reason_correct_candidate_lost: cand6 links student->advisor via students.advisor_instructor_id with no academic_advising join (score 100, tied); the scorer cannot penalise the spurious join, so the restricted variant was selected.
audit_reason: Adds an unrequested academic-advising appointment requirement, excluding otherwise qualifying assigned-advisor relationships.

question: List product categories sold in 2025 but not in 2024.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.category FROM products p JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.product_id = soi.product_id JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.order_id = so.order_id WHERE strftime('%Y', so.order_date) = '2025' AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM sales_order_items soi2 JOIN sales_orders so2 ON soi2.order_id = so2.order_id WHERE soi2.product_id = p.product_id AND strftime('%Y', so2.order_date) = '2024' )
reason_selected_wrong: Selected llm_sql_direct anti-joins at PRODUCT grain (NOT EXISTS same product_id in 2024), so a category still appears via its other products (score 100).
correct_candidate_sql: SELECT p.category FROM products p JOIN sales_order_items soi ON p.product_id = soi.product_id JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.order_id = so.order_id WHERE p.category IS NOT NULL GROUP BY p.category HAVING NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM sales_order_items soi2 JOIN sales_orders so2 ON soi2.order_id = so2.order_id JOIN products p2 ON soi2.product_id = p2.product_id WHERE p2.category = p.category AND strftime('%Y', so2.order_date) = '2024' ) AND EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM sales_order_items soi3 JOIN sales_orders so3 ON soi3.order_id = so3.order_id JOIN products p3 ON soi3.product_id = p3.product_id WHERE p3.category = p.category AND strftime('%Y', so3.order_date) = '2025' )
reason_correct_candidate_lost: cand6 anti-joins at CATEGORY grain (p2.category = p.category), the correct EXCEPT semantics, but returned 0 rows; the empty-result penalty dropped it to score 88, below the wrong product-grain candidate at 100. (Empty result is a data-dependent caveat; the set logic is correct.)
audit_reason: Excludes products sold in 2024 rather than excluding categories that were sold in 2024.

question: List patient identifiers that appear either in appointments or billing claims.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT "patients"."patient_id", "patients"."first_name" FROM "appointments" INNER JOIN "billing_claims" ON "appointments"."appointment_id" = "billing_claims"."appointment_id" INNER JOIN "patients" ON "appointments"."patient_id" = "patients"."patient_id"
reason_selected_wrong: Selected llm_primary INNER JOINs appointments to billing_claims, returning only patients in BOTH sources (score 84).
correct_candidate_sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.patient_id FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id UNION SELECT DISTINCT p.patient_id FROM patients p JOIN billing_claims b ON p.patient_id = b.patient_id
reason_correct_candidate_lost: cand2/cand4 use UNION of the two sources ('either'), 62 rows, but were scored only 59; the scorer under-valued the UNION set semantics versus the wrong INNER join.
audit_reason: Uses an appointment-to-claim inner join, returning only patients appearing in both sources.

question: Show each coach with total matches coached based on wins, losses, and draws.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT coach_id, first_name, last_name, wins, losses, draws FROM coaches
reason_selected_wrong: Selected candidate lists wins, losses, draws separately and never adds them.
no_correct_rationale: No Day 2 candidate computes wins + losses + draws; the paraphrased-addition obligation ('total matches ... based on wins, losses, and draws') was never generated.
audit_reason: Returns separate sums of wins, losses, and draws instead of adding them into total matches coached.

question: What is the total shipped gross profit from products supplied by low-risk suppliers, fulfilled by open warehouses, and sold on delivered orders?
day2_selected_sql: SELECT SUM(soi.line_total - (soi.quantity_shipped * p.standard_cost)) AS total_shipped_gross_profit FROM sales_order_items soi JOIN products p ON soi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN suppliers s ON p.supplier_id = s.supplier_id JOIN sales_orders so ON soi.order_id = so.order_id JOIN warehouses w ON soi.warehouse_id = w.warehouse_id WHERE s.risk_level = 'low' AND w.operating_status = 'open' AND so.order_status = 'delivered' AND soi.quantity_shipped > 0
reason_selected_wrong: Selected shipped-profit formula subtracts shipped-quantity cost from the FULL line_total revenue (mismatched basis).
no_correct_rationale: Every candidate uses full line_total (or line_subtotal) as revenue; none compute a shipped-revenue basis, so no candidate matches shipped gross profit.
audit_reason: The shipped-profit formula mixes full line revenue with shipped quantity and product standard cost.

question: What is the average numeric grade for students whose advising risk level is high?
day2_selected_sql: WITH high_risk_students AS ( SELECT DISTINCT student_id FROM academic_advising WHERE academic_risk_level = 'high' ), student_grades AS ( SELECT e.student_id, AVG(e.numeric_grade) AS avg_grade FROM enrollments e GROUP BY e.student_id ) SELECT AVG(sg.avg_grade) FROM student_grades sg JOIN high_risk_students hrs ON sg.student_id = hrs.student_id
reason_selected_wrong: Selected repair averages per-student averages equally instead of averaging the qualifying numeric-grade records.
no_correct_rationale: Qualifying candidates JOIN academic_advising and fanout-inflate the average (fatal), and the repair changes the grain; none compute a flat AVG over high-risk students' enrollment rows via EXISTS/IN.
audit_reason: Averages per-student averages equally instead of averaging the numeric-grade records belonging to high-risk students.

question: For each appointment type, calculate prescription cost per completed appointment.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT "appointments"."appointment_type", SUM("prescriptions"."estimated_cost") AS "total_prescription_cost", COUNT(*) AS "completed_appointment_count" FROM "appointments" LEFT JOIN "prescriptions" ON "appointments"."appointment_id" = "prescriptions"."appointment_id" WHERE "appointments"."appointment_status" = ? GROUP BY "appointments"."appointment_type"
reason_selected_wrong: Selected divides prescription cost by a denominator that is not the count of all completed appointments of the type.
no_correct_rationale: Ratio candidates INNER JOIN prescriptions (denominator = completed appointments that HAVE prescriptions) or use COUNT(*) over a LEFT JOIN; none divide by COUNT(DISTINCT all completed appointments).
audit_reason: Divides by completed appointments that have prescriptions, not all completed appointments of each type.

question: For each team, calculate injury games missed per active player.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT "teams"."team_id", "teams"."team_name", SUM("injuries"."games_missed") AS "total_games_missed", COUNT(*) AS "active_player_count" FROM "injuries" INNER JOIN "players" ON "injuries"."player_id" = "players"."player_id" INNER JOIN "teams" ON "injuries"."team_id" = "teams"."team_id" WHERE "players"."active_flag" = ? GROUP BY "teams"."team_id", "teams"."team_name"
reason_selected_wrong: Selected returns separate totals; the inner injuries join makes any per-player denominator cover only injured active players.
no_correct_rationale: The ratio candidates (cand4/5/6) INNER JOIN injuries, so the denominator counts only active players who have injuries, not all active players on the team; no candidate counts the full active roster.
audit_reason: The inner injury join makes the denominator include only active players who have injuries, not all active players on the team.

question: Show each student with the percentage of 120 credits they have completed.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT s.student_id, s.first_name, s.last_name, CAST(SUM(e.credits_earned) AS REAL) * 100.0 / 120.0 AS percentage_completed FROM students s JOIN enrollments e ON s.student_id = e.student_id GROUP BY s.student_id, s.first_name, s.last_name HAVING SUM(e.credits_earned) > 0
reason_selected_wrong: Selected divides a SUM of enrollment credits by 120 over a reduced population (INNER JOIN enrollments + HAVING SUM>0).
no_correct_rationale: Computing candidates use SUM(enrollments.credits_earned)/120 for only 55-69 of 100 students; non-computing candidates label raw credits. No candidate computes students.credits_earned/120 for every student. (Near-miss.)
audit_reason: Labels raw credits as a percentage and never divides credits earned by 120.

question: Show each program with department name and number of active students.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT p.program_name, d.department_name, COUNT(DISTINCT s.student_id) AS student_count FROM programs p JOIN departments d ON p.department_id = d.department_id JOIN students s ON p.program_id = s.program_id GROUP BY p.program_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT s.student_id) > 0
reason_selected_wrong: Selected repair counts all students per program and never filters to active students.
no_correct_rationale: The only candidates carrying the active-status filter (cand1/cand2) are malformed (stray raw student_id column, grain violation); the clean COUNT candidates omit the active filter. No candidate is both filtered and well-formed.
audit_reason: Counts all students in each program and never filters to active students.

question: What is the average number of completed matches per active season?
day2_selected_sql: SELECT AVG(match_count) AS average_matches_per_season FROM ( SELECT s.season_id, COUNT(m.match_id) AS match_count FROM seasons s JOIN matches m ON s.season_id = m.season_id GROUP BY s.season_id HAVING s.season_id IN ( SELECT season_id FROM seasons WHERE season_status = 'active' ) ) AS subquery
reason_selected_wrong: Selected counts every match in active seasons and never restricts matches to completed status.
no_correct_rationale: No Day 2 candidate adds a match-completed predicate; the completed-match filter was never generated.
audit_reason: Counts every match in active seasons and never filters matches to completed status.

question: How many active players have both a current contract and at least one completed transfer?
day2_selected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.player_id) FROM players p JOIN contracts c ON p.player_id = c.player_id JOIN transfers t ON p.player_id = t.player_id WHERE p.active_flag = '1' AND c.contract_status = 'active' AND t.transfer_id IS NOT NULL
reason_selected_wrong: Selected counts distinct active players with a current contract but only requires ANY transfer, not a completed transfer.
no_correct_rationale: No candidate combines COUNT(DISTINCT active player) with a transfer_status='completed' requirement; the one candidate carrying a transfer_status filter (cand2) is malformed (COUNT(*), no GROUP BY, stray column). Reclassified from the old Day1-pool label B: in the Day 2 pool no correct candidate exists.
audit_reason: Returns grouped player rows instead of one count and never requires transfer_status = 'completed'.

question: List customers who have never placed a delivered order.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name FROM customers c JOIN sales_orders so ON c.customer_id = so.customer_id WHERE NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM sales_orders so2 WHERE so2.customer_id = c.customer_id AND so2.order_status = 'delivered' ) GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
reason_selected_wrong: Selected anti-joins for delivered orders but the outer INNER JOIN sales_orders drops customers who have no orders.
no_correct_rationale: Near-miss split: cand4/5/6 hold the correct NOT EXISTS(delivered) but the spurious outer join; cand1 has the correct customers-only scope but omits the delivered predicate. No candidate combines both.
audit_reason: The inner join excludes customers with no orders, although they also have never placed a delivered order.

question: Show each student with the number of credits still needed to reach 120 credits.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT students.student_id, students.first_name, students.last_name, students.credits_earned, 120 - students.credits_earned AS credits_needed FROM students WHERE students.credits_earned < 120
reason_selected_wrong: Selected computes 120 - credits_earned but adds WHERE credits_earned < 120, dropping students at or above 120.
no_correct_rationale: Near-miss split: the subtracting candidates all add the <120 exclusion; the all-student candidates omit the subtraction. No candidate shows every student WITH the computed remainder.
audit_reason: Excludes students with 120 or more credits even though the request asks for each student.

question: List program identifiers that appear either in student records or course records.
day2_selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.program_id FROM programs p JOIN students s ON p.program_id = s.program_id JOIN courses c ON p.program_id = c.program_id
reason_selected_wrong: Selected INNER-joins students and courses, returning programs present in BOTH instead of either.
no_correct_rationale: Every Day 2 candidate uses INNER joins; none use UNION/OR to realise the 'either source' set semantics.
audit_reason: Uses inner joins, returning programs present in both students and courses instead of either source.
